evidence.scientific_clarity: All three numbered equations (ELBO eq.1, decomposition eq.2, retrieval-augmented eq.3) present with prose cross-referencing via \eqref; derivation chain is complete and readable.
evidence.typography: Latin Modern fonts (LMRoman, LMMathItalic, LMMathSymbols) used throughout — proper LaTeX math typography, no DejaVu Sans; all fonts embedded as Type 1 subsets with unicode mapping.
evidence.palette: No color used; appropriate for a math derivation block — no colorblind risk, no directional/categorical color misuse.
evidence.layout_density: Single letter-size page (612x792 pts) with equations and prose filling the space naturally; no gaping whitespace or overlapping labels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption \eqref references match equation labels; equations.tex (required output per brief) is absent from the working directory.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF produced by pdfTeX-1.40.29 with all 14 font subsets embedded (emb=yes, sub=yes, uni=yes); pdftotext extracts full text confirming real vector content.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (166KB), figure.png (250KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; equations.tex is missing despite being listed as a required output in the brief.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption identifies each equation by role (ELBO via Jensen, decomposition into reconstruction+KL, retrieval-augmented conditioning on k exemplars) with specific mechanistic claims.

overall_strengths: Excellent LaTeX math typesetting: Latin Modern fonts, proper \mathbb{E}/\mathcal{L}/\mathrm{KL} distinctions, embedded Type 1 fonts, and fully selectable vector PDF.
Caption is precise and informative, cross-referencing all three equations with specific mechanistic descriptions of each derivation step.
overall_weaknesses: equations.tex is missing from the working directory — the brief explicitly lists it as a required output alongside figure.pdf/png, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py.
No equations.tex means the derivation is not reproducible from source in the output directory; a reviewer cannot inspect or modify the raw LaTeX.
